content.text_content:
CENTRAL FLORIDA OPTOMETRY GROUP
880 Lake Baldwin Lane, Orlando, FL 32814
Phone: (407) 555-4410 | Fax: (407) 555-4411

Date: November 8, 2024

RE: GLAUCOMA EVALUATION REFERRAL

Patient Name: Patricia N. Okafor
Date of Birth: 02/18/1971 (53 years old)
Phone: (407) 555-6203

Dear Dr. Montzka,

I am referring Ms. Patricia Okafor for glaucoma evaluation after elevated intraocular pressure was found on routine examination.

HISTORY:
Ms. Okafor presented for a routine eye examination and updated glasses. She denies eye pain, halos, headaches, or subjective vision change. She has no prior ocular history and takes no eye medications.

EXAMINATION:
Visual acuity (corrected): OD 20/20, OS 20/20
Intraocular pressure (non-contact tonometry, 2:40 PM): OD 26 mmHg, OS 27 mmHg
Anterior segment: unremarkable; angles appear open by van Herick
Fundus: cup-to-disc ratio 0.55 both eyes, symmetric, rims intact

IMPRESSION:
Elevated intraocular pressure both eyes with symmetric cupping. Glaucoma suspect - please evaluate.

Thank you for seeing her. Please send your consultation report to our office.

Sincerely,

James Liu, OD
Central Florida Optometry Group

extracted_data.chief_complaint: Elevated IOP found on routine examination
impression: Glaucoma suspect - elevated IOP OU, symmetric cupping

content.text_content:
MONTZKA EYE CLINIC - OFFICE VISIT NOTE

Patient: Patricia N. Okafor
DOB: 02/18/1971
MRN: MEC-2024-0917
Date of Service: November 21, 2024
Visit Type: New Patient - Glaucoma Evaluation
Provider: Elena Montzka, MD

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Referred by optometry for elevated intraocular pressure found on routine examination.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Okafor is a 53-year-old woman referred after screening intraocular pressures of 26 and 27 mmHg. She is asymptomatic - no eye pain, halos, or subjective vision change. No prior ocular surgery or trauma. No systemic or topical corticosteroid use.

EXAMINATION:
Visual acuity (corrected): OD 20/20, OS 20/20
Applanation tonometry (9:20 AM): OD 24 mmHg, OS 25 mmHg
Central corneal thickness (pachymetry): OD 545 um, OS 548 um
Gonioscopy: angles open to ciliary body band 360 degrees both eyes, no synechiae
Optic discs: cup-to-disc ratio 0.55 both eyes, symmetric, neuroretinal rims intact, no notching or disc hemorrhage

IMAGING AND TESTING:
OCT retinal nerve fiber layer: average RNFL thickness within normal limits both eyes, no focal thinning.
Humphrey visual field 24-2: full fields both eyes, reliable indices.

ASSESSMENT:
1. Ocular hypertension, both eyes (H40.053) - intraocular pressure in the mid-20s with open angles, normal optic discs, full visual fields, and normal RNFL thickness.

PLAN:
1. Started latanoprost 0.005% one drop in both eyes at bedtime.
2. Return in four to five weeks for repeat intraocular pressure check on treatment.
3. Repeat visual fields and OCT RNFL in six months to establish baseline trend.

[ELECTRONIC SIGNATURE APPLIED]
Elena Montzka, MD
Dictated: 11/21/2024 16:05 | Signed: 11/21/2024 16:30

extracted_data.plan: Latanoprost OU nightly; IOP check in four to five weeks

findings.description: Retinal nerve fiber layer within normal limits; no focal thinning

summary.headline: Normal RNFL - no glaucomatous structural damage
key_findings: Average RNFL 96 microns - within normal limits
summary.clinical_impression: Normal baseline scan for ocular hypertension monitoring

soap.subjective: Referred after elevated intraocular pressure on routine optometric examination. Asymptomatic. Works night shifts; prefers a once-daily routine.
soap.objective: Applanation pressures elevated both eyes; open angles on gonioscopy; healthy discs, full fields, normal RNFL thickness.
soap.plan: Latanoprost 0.005% one drop both eyes at bedtime started; pressure re-check on treatment in four to five weeks.

appointments.note: First on-treatment pressure check; reinforce bedtime dosing routine.
